_note: Sample grounding data illustrating the structure used by the CommonKeys housing assistant. Listings and figures are demonstrative.

platform.name: CommonKeys
platform.mission: Make affordable, accessible, and sustainable housing attainable for low-income individuals and families in the Greater Toronto Area.
features: Community forum to connect residents and share resources

affordabilityLevels.description: Rent geared to income (typically ~30% of household income)
Below average market rent for the area
At or slightly below market rate with support programs available

financialAidPrograms.name: Canada-Ontario Housing Benefit (COHB)
financialAidPrograms.description: Portable monthly benefit to help pay rent, tied to the household rather than a unit.

financialAidPrograms.name: Rent-Geared-to-Income (RGI) Subsidy
financialAidPrograms.description: Rent set at approximately 30% of gross monthly household income.

financialAidPrograms.name: Toronto Rent Bank
financialAidPrograms.description: Interest-free loans and grants for tenants facing rent arrears or moving costs.

faq.q: Who is eligible for affordable housing?
faq.a: Eligibility generally depends on household income relative to area thresholds; each program lists its own criteria.
Browse listings, check the eligibility requirements for the development, and follow its application link. Financial aid programs can be applied for in parallel.
